text:
Market today: Markets are CLOSED today for the Juneteenth holiday - nothing trades, no action needed. Yesterday ended on a strong note (Nasdaq +1.9%, S&P +1%) on tech strength and easing Iran tensions. The one cloud: this week the Fed hinted it may raise rates again later in 2026. That matters for you because you use margin - higher rates make borrowed money cost more and tend to pressure tech/chip stocks the most.

Your holdings: All look fine today except one. PLTR ($128.47, you're down just $165 / -1.3%) has slipped below its average price line and is losing steam - worth watching; the plan would be to trim some near here and bail fully only if it falls under $85. Everything else is healthy: SMH (+$2,597), SNOW (+$3,147, your big winner), AVGO, SPY, QQQ, QQQI, CRWD all sit safely above their safety lines. SPCX (your brand-new, very volatile IPO) is up about $1,808 (+15%) - it's too new for the structure tools, so manage it by hand with a mental floor around $150.

Buy ideas: Nothing to do today since the market is closed, but a few clean chip setups are lining up for Monday if they hold: ARM (buy near $431, bail under $423 - best reward of the group), LRCX (buy near $388.65, bail under $386.43), and AMD (buy near $534, bail under $531). Only act if they're still set up when trading reopens.

Bottom line: Relax today - markets are shut. The only name worth a glance is PLTR, and keep the Fed's rate-hike hint in the back of your mind since you're on margin.

Details: C:\Users\NarendraGhuge\market-alerts\report.html - for my own decision, not financial advice.
